messages.content:
You are comparing two analyst responses to determine which better demonstrates knowledge of a specific finding from a longitudinal dataset.

Question asked: Are any user accounts showing access volume or scope anomalies relative to their peer group?

Finding to evaluate: data transfer volumes escalated progressively over a multi-week period with each successive week showing higher peak transfer sizes than the previous

Response A:
Yes. Across the week‑long data set a handful of users stand out for **far higher download volume and/or a much broader repository‑access scope than the rest of the team**.

| Day (episode) | User | Repos accessed | Volume (MB) | Why it is anomalous |
|---------------|------|----------------|------------|---------------------|
| 2024‑09‑11 (ep 010) | **jmorris** | 9 repos (archive/project‑nova‑2021, project‑sentinel/avionics, project‑atlas/firmware, project‑beacon/signal‑proc, project‑beacon/firmware‑legacy, project‑atlas/test‑harness, project‑atlas/schematics, project‑atlas/integration‑tests, project‑beacon/firmware‑legacy) | 231.7 MB | The only user that day with **≥ 8 repos**; volume is in the top‑quartile (most others ≤ 2‑4 repos, ≤ 180 MB). |
| 2024‑09‑12 (ep 011) | **jmorris** | 12 repos (archive/project‑nova‑2021, project‑beacon/signal‑proc, project‑beacon/firmware‑legacy, shared/documentation, project‑atlas/integration‑tests, project‑atlas/firmware, project‑atlas/test‑harness, project‑atlas/schematics, project‑beacon/rf‑modules, shared/toolchain, archive/project‑nova‑2021/specs, …) | 272.2 MB | Repo count again **well above the peer median (2‑5)** and download volume is the highest of the day. |
| 2024‑09‑13 (ep 012) | **jmorris** | 10 repos (project‑atlas/test‑harness, shared/documentation, project‑atlas/schematics, shared/toolchain, project‑beacon/rf‑modules, project‑sentinel/avionics, project‑atlas/integration‑tests, project‑beacon/signal‑proc, archive/project‑nova‑2021/specs, …) | 374.5 MB | **Largest volume** of any user that week and still accesses **double‑digit repo count**. |
| 2024‑09‑11 (ep 010) | **tchen** | 2 repos | 175.3 MB | Volume is in the top‑10 % while the repo count is typical; the amount of data moved is unusually high for a 2‑repo user. |
| 2024‑09‑12 (ep 011) | **rjohnson** | 5 repos | 164.8 MB | Accesses more repos than the average (≈ 3) and downloads a large amount of data. |
| 2024‑09‑05 (ep 004) | **lpatel** | 3 repos | 177.4 MB | Volume exceeds the median (≈ 120 MB) despite a modest repo count. |
| 2024‑09‑06 (ep 005) | **kwilson** | 2 repos | 150.8 MB | One of the highest download volumes for a 2‑repo user. |
| 2024‑09‑06 (ep 005) | **rjohnson** | 2 repos | 158.6 MB | Same pattern as kwilson – high volume with few repos. |
| 2024‑09‑10 (ep 009) | **sbrown** | 2 repos | 168.5 MB | Very high data transfer for a minimal repo set. |

**Interpretation**

* **jmorris** is the clearest outlier: on three separate days he accesses **9‑12 repositories** and downloads **> 200 MB**, far exceeding the typical 2‑5 repos and ≤ 180 MB seen for most peers. This pattern suggests a **scope anomaly** (broader repository footprint) and a **volume anomaly** (large data movement).  
* Several other users (tchen, rjohnson, kwilson, lpatel, sbrown) show **volume anomalies**—they download > 150 MB while only touching 2‑3 repos, which is atypical compared to the bulk of the team.  

These deviations merit further investigation to determine whether they reflect legitimate work (e.g., large‑binary engineering assets) or potential insider‑threat behavior.  

**Citations**

* jmorris on 2024‑09‑11: 9 repos, 231.7 MB 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】  
* jmorris on 2024‑09‑12: 12 repos, 272.2 MB 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_011】  
* jmorris on 2024‑09‑13: 10 repos, 374.5 MB 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_012】  
* tchen on 2024‑09‑11: 2 repos, 175.3 MB 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】  
* rjohnson on 2024‑09‑12: 5 repos, 164.8 MB 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_011】  
* lpatel on 2024‑09‑05: 3 repos, 177.4 MB 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_004】  
* kwilson on 2024‑09‑06: 2 repos, 150.8 MB 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_005】  
* rjohnson on 2024‑09‑06: 2 repos, 158.6 MB 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_005】  
* sbrown on 2024‑09‑10: 2 repos, 168.5 MB 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_009】

Response B:
**Yes – a handful of user accounts stand out when their download‑volume and/or the breadth of repositories they touch are compared to the rest of the team.**  

Below is a concise “peer‑group” view (average ≈ 20‑30 files, 80‑150 MB, 2‑4 repos per day) and the accounts that deviate from that norm, with the exact log entries that expose the out‑of‑band behaviour.

| User | Day / Entry | Files Downloaded | Volume (MB) | Repos Accessed | Why it is anomalous |
|------|-------------|------------------

Which response better demonstrates awareness of this finding? The response may use different terminology. Focus on whether the core insight is present, not exact wording.

Respond with ONLY one word: A, B, or TIE
/no_think
